message: agent2 here: implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() in src/datasets/features/features.py. Will touch the update() method only. Will ping before finalizing.

agent1: Implemented keep_features_dicts_synced decorator in src/datasets/features/features.py (Features class) to auto-sync _column_requires_decoding on __setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear. Only touched features.py, no test file changes.

Heads up: my keep_features_dicts_synced decorator replaces update() with 'update = keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update)' (plain dict.update semantics, no deep merge) so our changes to update() will conflict. Can you implement deep-merge as a wrapper around the synced dict.update, e.g. define a custom update() method that does the deep-merge logic then calls self._column_requires_decoding recompute (or reuses my keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on your custom method)? Let me know when you push so I can merge cleanly.

agent2: heads up, our features overlap on Features.update() — I already modified update() in features.py to deep-merge dict values (recursive merge when both current & new values are dicts). Your decorator wrapping update()/__setitem__/etc for _column_requires_decoding sync should be compatible as long as it wraps around my update() body rather than replacing its logic. team/agent1 shows no commits ahead of team/main yet on my end - push when ready and I'll merge before final patch.

agent2: conflict — you set update = keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update), which drops my deep-merge logic in update(). I'll resolve by keeping your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator/pattern but wrapping it around my custom update() method body (deep-merge) instead of dict.update, so both features coexist. Will push the merged result.

Merged your team/agent2 branch into agent1 — resolved the update() conflict by keeping your deep-merge logic but having it assign via self[key]=... (routes through my synced __setitem__), so both features coexist. Pushed to team/agent1. Tests pass.

agent2: merged and pushed. Resolved conflict by renaming your update=keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update) target to a new _update() method containing my deep-merge logic, still wrapped with keep_features_dicts_synced. All existing tests pass. patch.txt built from team/main..HEAD (merged tree) contains both our changes.
